---
title: "Instagram"
platform_slug: "instagram"
sources:
  ugc:
    BR: data/global/instagram/ugc.yml
    EU: data/global/instagram/ugc.yml
    UK: data/global/instagram/ugc.yml
  ads:
    BR: data/regional/BR/meta/ads.yml
    EU: data/regional/EU/meta/ads.yml
    UK: data/regional/UK/meta/ads.yml
---

## Transparency Assessment


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path
project_root = Path.cwd()
while not (project_root / 'utils').exists() and project_root != project_root.parent:
    project_root = project_root.parent
if str(project_root) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(project_root))
from utils.responses_aggregator import generate_platform_question_sections
```

```{python}
#| label: platform-summary
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from utils.platform_summary import generate_platform_summary
generate_platform_summary("instagram")
```

Instagram was assessed under both the UGC and ADS frameworks across three regions (BR, EU, and UK). For UGC, a single global dataset is applied to all regions: all four special criteria (UGC_SC1–SC4, which together carry 75% of the score weight) received negative or heavily restricted answers, as the `Meta Content Library API` does not permit structured data extraction to users' own infrastructure and limits access to accounts meeting follower thresholds or holding verification status; UGC_SC4 is the sole partial exception, acknowledging a researcher-facing interface that allows some extraction but only for approved academics. All remaining UGC criteria scored negative, yielding a score in the Irrelevant band and rendering Instagram's user-generated content essentially inaccessible for systematic research. For advertising transparency, the picture varies by jurisdiction: in the EU and UK the `Meta Ad Library API` provides full programmatic access to ads of any type (AD_SC1 and AD_SC3 scored full), whereas in Brazil access via the same API is limited to political and issue ads (AD_SC1 scored partial). GUI-based structured extraction is unavailable in the EU (AD_SC2 = no), and partial in Brazil and the UK. The library consistently provides advertiser identifiers, ad period data, removal reasons, and audience reach figures across all regions; persistent gaps include the absence of user engagement data, incomplete targeting-criteria disclosure, missing spending granularity, and no rate-limit documentation specific to the Ad Library. Overall, Instagram presents a strongly asymmetric transparency posture: advertising disclosure is considerably more developed than UGC access, and even the former remains constrained in Brazil relative to the EU and UK.

## Detailed Assessment


## User-Generated Content

```{python}
#| label: ugc-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="instagram",
    question_type="ugc",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## Advertising

```{python}
#| label: ads-questions
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
generate_platform_question_sections(
    platform="instagram",
    question_type="ads",
    year="2025",
    heading_level=3,
)
```

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
